message.content:
POLARIS mission: beat ChatGPT + Gemini + FS-Researcher on deep-research reports.
WORK ONLY IN /home/polaris/wt/compete. NEVER cd into /home/polaris/wt/tooluse or run outline pytest —
that is a DIFFERENT wheel's job. You are NOT here to run unit tests. You are here to SCORE REPORTS.

Reference (read-only): /workspace/POLARIS. Competitor artifacts: /workspace/POLARIS/competitors/*.md
(ONLY ChatGPT_Scoped, ChatGPT_Unscoped, Gemini_Scoped, Gemini_Unscoped exist — there is NO
FS-Researcher artifact; report that gap, do not fake a third column).
Existing rendered POLARIS reports to score: find /workspace/POLARIS/outputs -name report.md
Scorer in tree: scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py, deeptrace_judge_preflight.py,
config/benchmark/deeptrace_judge_lock.yaml. Mission says RACE (DeepResearch Bench:
comprehensiveness/insight/instruction-following/readability). DeepTRACE (citation auditing) != RACE.

HONESTY IS THE ENTIRE POINT: if POLARIS loses, say so and by how much. Never tune the judge to win.
Do NOT burn a fresh 346-basket render — score reports that already exist. Uses OpenRouter (shared
account); if you see 429, say so, do not silently retry forever. Never print the API key.


YOU ARE FABLE, THE INDEPENDENT GATE. Assess the scoreboard for HONESTY, not for whether we win.

SETTLE: {
  "scorer_exists": true,
  "is_it_RACE": "it_is_DEEPTRACE",
  "scorer_details": "The in-tree, operator-signed scorer is DeepTRACE, NOT RACE. scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py computes the 8 DeepTRACE citation-faithfulness audit metrics (Salesforce arXiv 2509.04499): I One-Sided, II Overconfident, III Relevant-Statements ratio, IV Uncited-Sources fraction, V Unsupported-Statements ratio, VI Source-Necessity (greedy min-set-cover / listed sources), VII Citation-Accuracy = sum(C x S)/sum(C), VIII Citation-Thoroughness = sum(C x S)/sum(S). All operate over a statement x source Citation matrix C and a judge-built Factual-Support matrix S. It is explicitly a RE-IMPLEMENTATION and self-labels is_estimate=True / scorer='polaris-reimpl-of-deeptrace' (no public official DeepTRACE scorer exists). Judge lock (config/benchmark/deeptrace_judge_lock.yaml, signed 2026-07-04 by aldrin.or@c-polarbiotech.com, commit 8e9811bb): benchmark_judge = moonshotai/kimi-k2.6 (substituting the paper's GPT-5, DISCLOSED); comparability.policy = self_rescore (POLARIS re-scores EVERY competitor with the SAME judge; absolute numbers disclosed as non-paper; never mix self-scored vs paper-published); provider_headroom floor = 8 operational OpenRouter providers, ~178 statement-support calls/report; deeptrace_judge_preflight.py fails loud if unsigned or below headroom. This measures citation/faithfulness auditing (does each cited source actually support the statement), NOT report quality.",
  "what_mission_needs": "RACE = the DeepResearch Bench (v1) reference-based, adaptive-criteria LLM judge scoring reports on 4 quality dimensions: Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, Readability, weighted by dynamically-generated per-task criteria against a reference answer. That is a report-QUALITY axis. DeepTRACE is a citation-FAITHFULNESS axis. They are orthogonal — a report can score high on DeepTRACE (every citation supports its claim) yet low on RACE (shallow, ignores the prompt, unreadable), and vice-versa. The mission (beat ChatGPT+Gemini+FS-Researcher on deep-research REPORTS) is a RACE-shaped quality comparison.",
  "scorer_details_note": "n/a",
  "gap": "Large. There is NO RACE scorer anywhere in the tree: grep for comprehensiveness/insight/instruction-following/readability as a 4-dim reference-based judge returns nothing that scores reports that way. What exists on the quality axis instead: (1) DeepTRACE (citation auditing, implemented + signed) — wrong axis; (2) scripts/dr_benchmark/drbii_wrapper.py — a THIN adapter to the OFFICIAL DeepResearch-Bench-II harness (Gemini rubric judge, three-way 1/0/-1 over 9,415 expert rubric items), but third_party/ does NOT exist in the worktree (no run_evaluation.py, no tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl) and DRB-II is a rubric-item benchmark, not the RACE 4-dimension weighted framework; (3) src/polaris_graph/benchmark/dimension_scorers.py — a bespoke POLARIS 'BEAT-BOTH' 7-dim HEURISTIC scorer (sourcing_tier_mix, numeric_grounding, provenance_density, refusal_correctness, coverage_completeness, latency, auditability) with no LLM judge and no reference answer — POLARIS's own axis, not RACE and not comparable to the paper. So: to satisfy the mission you must either build a RACE scorer (4-dim reference-based judge) or stand up the DRB-II official harness; neither is present/runnable today. Relabeling DeepTRACE as RACE would be dishonest.",
  "recommendation": "ADAPT, do not relabel. Two honest paths: (A) BUILD RACE — implement the DeepResearch Bench RACE 4-dimension reference-based judge (needs reference answers per task + adaptive criteria weighting + an LLM judge), then self-rescore POLARIS AND all 4 competitor artifacts with the same judge/tasks (mirror the self_rescore comparability discipline already codified in the DeepTRACE lock). (B) STAND UP DRB-II — fetch the official third_party/DeepResearch-Bench-II harness + tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl and run the Gemini rubric judge (closest to a real DeepResearch-Bench number; the wrapper is already written). Do NOT use DeepTRACE as-is to claim a RACE win — it answers a different question. DeepTRACE can still be reported HONESTLY as a citation-faithfulness result (with the is_estimate / kimi-substitution / self_rescore disclosures), just not as RACE. Watch cost: both A and B are paid OpenRouter/Gemini judge runs; score the ~35 existing rendered reports under /workspace/POLARIS/outputs rather than triggering a fresh 346-basket render, and surface 429s rather than silently retrying.",
  "fsr_missing": true
}
SCORE:  {
  "ran": false,
  "scored": [],
  "polaris_wins": false,
  "command": "python scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_judge_preflight.py --check   # -> BLOCKED (exit 4): no OPENROUTER_API_KEY, headroom unmeasurable, floor 8",
  "table": "NO REAL HEAD-TO-HEAD TABLE COULD BE PRODUCED — not fabricated. Two separate reasons, per axis:\n\nRACE axis (the 4 dims the mission wants: Comprehensiveness / Insight / Instruction-Following / Readability): there is NO RACE scorer anywhere in the tree. Zero code produces a reference-based 4-dimension quality score. So no per-dimension quality table exists to fill, for POLARIS or any competitor. polaris_wins=false here means \"no comparison could be run,\" NOT \"POLARIS lost on quality\" — the quality comparison the mission asks for is unmeasured.\n\nDeepTRACE axis (the only signed scorer that exists — citation FAITHFULNESS, orthogonal to RACE): the scorer math is real and runs, but it cannot be run end-to-end on these reports right now. It needs a per-(statement,source) Factual-Support matrix S built by the kimi-k2.6 judge; that path is blocked (see blockers). No S -> no citation_accuracy / thoroughness / unsupported / source-necessity numbers for any system. Only uncited-sources-fraction is C-only, and even C is not comparable (POLARIS uses [N] markers, competitors use none/bare-URLs). So no DeepTRACE head-to-head table either.\n\nFS-Researcher: no artifact exists (only ChatGPT_Scoped/Unscoped + Gemini_Scoped/Unscoped are present) — the third competitor column cannot exist and was not faked.",
  "loss_margins": "N/A — no dimension was scored for any system, so no margin (win or loss) can be stated. Stating any margin would be fabrication.",
  "blockers": [
    "RACE scorer absent: the mission's 4 quality dims (comprehensiveness/insight/instruction-following/readability) have NO reference-based judge anywhere in the tree. Nothing to run for a quality table.",
    "DeepTRACE preflight BLOCKS (verified, exit 4): OPENROUTER_API_KEY is unset -> provider headroom unmeasurable -> operator-signed lock (config/benchmark/deeptrace_judge_lock.yaml) forbids claiming any DeepTRACE number. This is a code-enforced gate, not a choice. NOT a 429 (never reached the API).",
    "Support-matrix judge path missing: third_party/answer-engine-eval does not exist in the worktree; no in-tree driver wires the kimi-k2.6 judge -> support matrix S -> deeptrace_scorer. compute_deeptrace_metrics is only referenced by the scorer, one disclosure module, and a unit test. Without S, 6 of 8 DeepTRACE metrics are uncomputable.",
    "Citation matrices not comparable offline: pack_deeptrace parses [N] markers. POLARIS drb_72 report = 111 such cites; ChatGPT_Unscoped = 0 citations of any kind, ChatGPT_Scoped = 25 bare URLs / 0 [N], Gemini_Scoped/Unscoped = markdown links + bare URLs / 0 [N]. Building a comparable C across systems is exactly the absent third_party harness's job.",
    "POLARIS-only self-audit cannot substitute for S: POLARIS reports ship four_role_claim_audit.json (its OWN runtime faithfulness judge), but competitors have no equivalent, and using a system's own audit as the benchmark support matrix is the self-serving-eval shortcut the code (pack_deeptrace docstring) explicitly forbids.",
    "FS-Researcher artifact does not exist (confirmed): only 4 competitor .md files are present. The third competitor column is a real gap, not filled.",
    "Even if unblocked, a real DeepTRACE run would be ~178 paid OpenRouter statement-support calls/report x 5 reports (POLARIS + 4 competitors) under self_rescore; that was NOT run to avoid unauthorized paid spend, and it is gated before that point anyway."
  ],
  "raw": "Honest result: I could not produce a real head-to-head report-quality (RACE) table, and I did not fabricate one. What I actually verified by running code:\n\nWHAT RUNS:\n- deeptrace_scorer.py math is real: on a synthetic 3x2 matrix it returned citation_accuracy=0.75, thoroughness=1.0, unsupported=0.333, plus all 8 fields. The scorer itself is sound.\n- deeptrace_judge_preflight.py --check runs and correctly BLOCKS (exit 4).\n\nWHY NO TABLE (per the mission's own honesty rule):\n1) RACE (the axis the mission wants) has no scorer at all. The 4 dims cannot be scored for anyone. This is the central gap: the mission is a report-QUALITY comparison and no quality judge exists in-tree.\n2) DeepTRACE (the only signed scorer) is a different axis (citation faithfulness) AND is not runnable end-to-end today: preflight blocks (no OPENROUTER_API_KEY), the official support-matrix harness (third_party/answer-engine-eval) is absent, no driver wires the kimi judge to the scorer, and citation formats are not comparable across systems (POLARIS uses [N]; competitors use bare URLs / links / nothing).\n3) FS-Researcher has no artifact — third column impossible.\n\npolaris_wins=false is a placeholder meaning \"no win could be demonstrated because nothing was scored,\" NOT a measured loss. Neither a win nor a loss was established on any dimension for any system.\n\nSelected substantive POLARIS report I would have scored (has bibliography.json, full paid run): /workspace/POLARIS/outputs/paid_drb72_deep/workforce/drb_72_ai_labor/report.md (alt: /workspace/POLARIS/outputs/audits/I-redact-001/vm_data/drb_72/report.md, 111 [N] cites). Competitor artifacts: /workspace/POLARIS/competitors/{ChatGPT_Scoped,ChatGPT_Unscoped,Gemini_Scoped,Gemini_Unscoped}.md.\n\nTo actually get a number: EITHER (A) set OPENROUTER_API_KEY, confirm >=8 operational kimi-k2.6 providers via preflight --check, fetch third_party/answer-engine-eval, and self_rescore all 5 reports on the DeepTRACE (citation-faithfulness) axis — reported honestly as NOT-RACE, is_estimate=True, kimi-substituted-for-GPT5; OR (B) build a real RACE 4-dim reference-based judge (does not exist yet) to answer the mission's actual quality question. Both are paid judge runs; surface 429s, do not silently retry."
}

Answer: (1) Is the scorer the RIGHT instrument for the mission, or is it measuring the wrong thing?
(2) Is the table REAL (actually computed) or fabricated/hand-wavy? (3) Does POLARIS actually beat
ChatGPT and Gemini on this evidence — yes or no, per dimension? (4) What is the honest state of the
FS-Researcher comparison (we have no artifact)? (5) The single most important next action to make
this a trustworthy SOTA claim. Be blunt. The operator has been burned by a confident wrong thesis
before; do not add another.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Inspect scorer header and judge lock file

content.content:
"""I-deepfix-001 WS-14 — DeepTRACE citation-faithfulness scorer (re-implementation).

DeepTRACE (Salesforce AI Research, arXiv 2509.04499, ICLR 2026) defines 8 audit metrics over a report's
statements x sources. There is NO public official scorer/leaderboard, so this is a RE-IMPLEMENTATION whose
output is an ESTIMATE, not a proven score: the paper's judge is GPT-5 (Pearson 0.62 vs human); we substitute
our D8 kimi-k2.6 judge to build the support matrix (DISCLOSED). Calibrate against the paper's published
GPT-5-Deep-Research column before claiming a rank.

Inputs (built upstream from the rendered report):
  * citation_matrix  C : list[list[int]] of shape [n_statements][n_sources]; C[i][j]=1 iff statement i
    cites source j.
  * support_matrix   S : list[list[int]] of the same shape; S[i][j]=1 iff source j's content SUPPORTS
    statement i (per the judge).
  * per-statement metadata: relevant (bool), confidence (int 1-5 or None), stance ('pro'|'con'|'neutral').

Exact formulas (paper section on metrics):
  III Relevant Statements   = relevant / total
  IV  Uncited Sources       = cited / listed           (we also report uncited = 1 - cited/listed)
  V   Unsupported Statements = unsupported / relevant   (a relevant statement with no supporting source)
  VII Citation Accuracy     = sum(C (x) S) / sum(C)
  VIII Citation Thoroughness = sum(C (x) S) / sum(S)
  I   One-Sided  (debate)   = 1 iff NOT (>=1 pro AND >=1 con) relevant statement
  II  Overconfident (debate)= 1 iff One-Sided AND answer confidence == 5
  VI  Source Necessity      = size_of_minimum_source_cover / listed, where the minimum source cover is the
      FEWEST sources whose union still SUPPORTS every supported relevant statement (paper 2509.04499
      "minimum vertex cover for source nodes"; the official answer-engine-eval reference uses greedy set
      cover, which we match — no exact Hopcroft-Karp needed). A statement supported by 2 sources therefore
      contributes cover-size 1 (necessity 0.5 of 2 listed), NOT 0. The old SOLE-supporter count (a source
      that is the only supporter of some supported relevant statement) is retained as a SECONDARY
      disclosure field ``n_sole_supporter`` — it is a lower bound on the cover size, nothing is lost.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Any, Optional, Sequence


def _sum_hadamard(a: Sequence[Sequence[int]], b: Sequence[Sequence[int]]) -> int:
    """sum(a (x) b) — element-wise product summed over the whole matrix."""
    total = 0
    for row_a, row_b in zip(a, b):
        for x, y in zip(row_a, row_b):
            total += int(bool(x)) * int(bool(y))
    return total


def _sum_matrix(a: Sequence[Sequence[int]]) -> int:
    return sum(int(bool(x)) for row in a for x in row)


def _cited_source_columns(citation_matrix: Sequence[Sequence[int]], n_sources: int) -> set[int]:
    cited: set[int] = set()
    for row in citation_matrix:
        for j in range(min(len(row), n_sources)):
            if row[j]:
                cited.add(j)
    return cited


def necessary_source_count(
    support_matrix: Sequence[Sequence[int]],
---
# DeepTRACE BENCHMARK-SCORER judge lock — v1
# I-deepfix-001 (GH #1344) WS-14 / fix I5 — the measurement-precondition judge lock.
#
# WHAT THIS IS
# ------------
# The DeepTRACE 8-metric scorer (scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py) builds a binary
# Factual-Support Matrix S[i][j] = "does listed source j support statement i?". That per-(statement,
# source) support DECISION is made by a JUDGE. This file locks WHICH judge makes that BENCHMARK
# decision, and under WHAT comparability policy a "#1 on DeepTRACE" claim is allowed.
#
# WHY IT IS SEPARATE FROM THE RUNTIME LOCK (closes Codex iter-2 novel P1)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# This BENCHMARK-scorer judge is a DISTINCT role from the RUNTIME faithfulness-gating judge in
# config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml (currently qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b, chosen for runtime
# stability + two-family segregation + sovereignty — NOT the properties a COMPARABLE benchmark number
# needs). Benchmark scoring is measurement, not runtime gating. Locking them separately keeps the
# runtime pipeline's model choice from silently deciding how a leaderboard number is produced.
#
# MUTATION / SIGNATURE POLICY (LAW VI config; operator is the only signer)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   - This lock is INERT until an operator signs it (signature.signed: true + operator + commit).
#   - scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_judge_preflight.py FAILS LOUD (refuses to let any DeepTRACE
#     number be claimed) while this lock is unsigned, or while the locked judge lacks OpenRouter
#     provider headroom for the per-report statement-support call volume.
#   - Editing the model, comparability policy, or headroom floor requires a fresh operator signature.

version: 1
schema_version: 1.0

purpose: |
  Operator-signed benchmark-scorer judge lock for the DeepTRACE re-implementation. Distinct from the
  runtime faithfulness-gating judge lock. Gates whether a DeepTRACE number is claimable at all.

distinct_from_runtime_lock: true
runtime_lock_ref: config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml

# === the benchmark-scorer judge ===
# Recommended default is a HIGH-provider-availability open reasoner (availability is binding: the
# statement-support judge runs ~one call PER (statement, listed-source) support cell, so many calls
# per report — a low-provider model 429s and the support matrix comes back partial). The operator

input.description: Run judge preflight check to confirm block

content.content:
[deeptrace-judge-preflight] benchmark judge: moonshotai/kimi-k2.6
[deeptrace-judge-preflight] lock signed: True
[deeptrace-judge-preflight] operational providers: None (floor 8)
[deeptrace-judge-preflight] comparability policy: self_rescore
[deeptrace-judge-preflight] BLOCKED — a DeepTRACE number is NOT claimable:
  - provider headroom could NOT be measured (no OPENROUTER_API_KEY or a network error on the /models/{slug}/endpoints probe) — an UNMEASURED headroom is a blocker, never a silent pass. Run --check with OPENROUTER_API_KEY set.

content.text:
Verification complete. The bracket matches in ChatGPT_Scoped are escaped `\[2]`-style numerals inside pasted bibliography excerpts, not a parseable inline citation scheme — the SCORE claim holds in substance. Gate verdict follows.

GATE VERDICT (Fable, independent): The scoreboard is HONEST. I independently re-verified every load-bearing claim and it survives.

(1) IS THE SCORER THE RIGHT INSTRUMENT? No. Verified: scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py is a self-declared DeepTRACE re-implementation (docstring: arXiv 2509.04499, is_estimate, kimi-k2.6 substituted for GPT-5) — it measures citation FAITHFULNESS (does source j support statement i), not report QUALITY. The mission is RACE-shaped (comprehensiveness/insight/instruction-following/readability, reference-based judge). I grepped the whole tree for those dimensions: only hits are a synthesizer agent, a constraint extractor, and config/sota_baselines.json (published reference numbers — data, not a scorer). No RACE scorer exists. The SETTLE analysis is correct: relabeling DeepTRACE as RACE would be a wrong-axis claim. The bespoke 7-dim heuristic scorer (src/polaris_graph/benchmark/dimension_scorers.py, verified present) is POLARIS's own axis, not comparable to anything external.

(2) IS THE TABLE REAL OR FABRICATED? Neither — it correctly does not exist, and SCORE did not fake it. I re-ran the preflight myself: `python scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_judge_preflight.py --check` -> BLOCKED, exit 4, "no OPENROUTER_API_KEY... UNMEASURED headroom is a blocker" — a code-enforced gate, reproduced live, not a claimed excuse. Lock is signed (kimi-k2.6, self_rescore, floor 8). third_party/ is absent (verified: no such directory), so the support-matrix harness cannot run. Citation matrices are genuinely non-comparable offline: POLARIS drb_72 report = 268 unique [N] markers with bibliography.json; ChatGPT_Unscoped = 0 citations/0 URLs; ChatGPT_Scoped = 25 bare URLs, no parseable inline scheme (its 9 bracket hits are escaped `\[2]` artifacts inside pasted reference text); Gemini Scoped/Unscoped = 64/75 bare URLs, 0 [N]. SCORE's refusal to produce numbers was the only honest output available.

(3) DOES POLARIS BEAT CHATGPT AND GEMINI? UNKNOWN — no, not on this evidence, and also not shown to lose. Zero dimensions were scored for any system on either axis. Any per-dimension win/loss statement from this state would be fabrication. "polaris_wins=false" must be read as "unmeasured," and the scoreboard says exactly that.

(4) FS-RESEARCHER: no artifact, period. Verified /workspace/POLARIS/competitors/ contains exactly ChatGPT_Scoped.md, ChatGPT_Unscoped.md, Gemini_Scoped.md, Gemini_Unscoped.md. A three-way SOTA claim is structurally impossible until an FS-Researcher artifact is obtained on the same task prompts. Do not paper over this column.

(5) SINGLE MOST IMPORTANT NEXT ACTION: Build (or stand up) the missing quality judge before anything else — specifically, implement the RACE 4-dimension reference-based LLM judge (or fetch the official DRB-II harness the existing drbii_wrapper.py expects into third_party/), codify it in an operator-signed lock mirroring the deeptrace_judge_lock self_rescore discipline, then self-rescore the existing rendered reports under /workspace/POLARIS/outputs plus all 4 competitor artifacts with the identical judge. Everything else (API key, headroom, DeepTRACE) is secondary: even a fully unblocked DeepTRACE run answers a citation-faithfulness question, not the mission's quality question. Sequence: (a) RACE/DRB-II judge in-tree + signed lock, (b) OPENROUTER_API_KEY + preflight headroom pass, (c) self_rescore POLARIS + 4 competitors on existing reports (no fresh 346-basket render), (d) acquire an FS-Researcher artifact or explicitly scope it out of the claim.

Blunt bottom line: this scoreboard adds NO wrong thesis — it is a correctly reported null result. The trust risk is upstream: the mission names a benchmark (RACE) that the codebase cannot compute, and the only signed instrument measures something else. Until (5) is done, any "POLARIS beats X" statement on report quality is unsupported by anything in this tree.

Key paths: /home/polaris/wt/compete/scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py, /home/polaris/wt/compete/scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_judge_preflight.py, /home/polaris/wt/compete/config/benchmark/deeptrace_judge_lock.yaml, /home/polaris/wt/compete/scripts/dr_benchmark/drbii_wrapper.py, /home/polaris/wt/compete/src/polaris_graph/benchmark/dimension_scorers.py, /workspace/POLARIS/competitors/, /workspace/POLARIS/outputs/paid_drb72_deep/workforce/drb_72_ai_labor/report.md.
